footer.headline: Turnero for clinics that need a product story, not a loose stack
footer.deck: Turnero connects kiosk, waiting-room display, reception, mobile check-in, and site metrics without turning the pilot into an enterprise rollout.

columns.title: Integrations
lines: QR, thermal printer, and browser-based TV display
Web booking, webhooks, and queue API

landing.title: Turnero for clinics | kiosk + display + web
landing.description: Turnero software for growing clinics with kiosk, waiting-room display, reception, and web booking connected as one operating product.
landing.heading: Turnero for clinics with kiosk, waiting-room display, reception, and web booking

hero.title: The turnero that connects arrival, reception, room flow, and clinic management in one product story.
hero.deck: Aurora Derm is the pilot clinic we can demonstrate today: kiosk, reception, operator, waiting-room display, and dashboard all read from the same state. That makes the purchase feel like a product, not a collection of disconnected screens.

metrics.label: pilot clinic live today
metrics.detail: The same state is visible in landing, check-in, waiting room, and dashboard.

metrics.label: operates from one queue logic
metrics.detail: Admin and operator read the same queue instead of separate consoles.

metrics.label: buys against visible proof
metrics.detail: Wait time, no-show, and room load come from the same flow shown to the buyer.

panels.title: QR, kiosk, and mobile check-in tell the same arrival story
panels.copy: Patients enter oriented before they touch the counter.

panels.title: Admin and operator coordinate over the same queue
panels.copy: Front desk stops improvising between separate windows and separate states.

panels.title: The room understands what is happening without losing privacy
panels.copy: Display and private queue status show the same live call in language patients can follow.

panels.title: Leadership sees a buying reason, not just a visual refresh
panels.copy: The management readout comes from real pilot usage, not from a separate report.

focus.title: Three readings of the same product so the sale is easier to explain.
focus.deck: You do not need to promise a different system to every person. The same base solves daily operations, improves the patient experience, and leaves clear numbers behind.

cards.title: Operate without changing context
cards.copy: Everything critical happens on the same state model: arrivals, calls, reprints, closures, and traceability.
items: Fewer mental steps during peak hours
Shortcuts and control by room or station
A clearer console for active front desk work

cards.title: Understand arrival and waiting
cards.copy: Patients confirm whether they are scheduled, track their turn, and know what to do without depending on the counter.
items: QR, kiosk, and phone on the same queue
Fewer repeated questions in the room
More clarity before entering the consult room

cards.title: See why the suite is worth renewing
cards.copy: Operations leave useful trace data behind: wait time, no-show, and room load for decisions with context.
items: A dashboard ready for owner or manager
Reading by site, time window, and consult room
A cleaner argument for expanding the suite

modules.title: These surfaces prove the product already exists outside the deck.
modules.deck: They are not annexes. They are the pilot itself: arrival, reception, operator, and room can already be shown as one product.

cards.title: Reception runs the queue without changing context
cards.copy: Call, reprint, complete, and coordinate the room from the console a clinic can open live in front of the buyer.

cards.title: The consult room runs with numpad shortcuts and a visible next step
cards.copy: A lighter surface for calling, re-calling, and closing without opening the full admin.
bullets: Readiness visible before the first call
Clear fallback if network or runtime degrade

cards.title: The kiosk resolves arrival and leaves the queue ready
cards.copy: Scheduled and walk-in patients enter the same route and leave reception with less manual load.
bullets: Scheduled / walk-in inside the same flow

cards.title: The room shows the right call and the next step
cards.copy: Waiting-room display and private queue status read the same queue so patients do not depend on the front desk.
bullets: Ticket, initials, and room with privacy
Hierarchy tuned for a real waiting room

journeys.title: The fixed story for the March 17 product presentation.
journeys.deck: Arrival, reception, room, and management. That order makes the product easier to understand and keeps Aurora Derm as the pilot clinic, not the brand that absorbs the suite.

lanes.title: The patient arrives oriented before they touch the counter
steps: Reminder, QR, or direct link all land in the same queue.
The kiosk absorbs scheduled and walk-in arrival without inventing two systems.
Reception receives a ready case instead of starting from zero.

lanes.title: Reception and consult room operate on the same state
steps: Admin and operator read the same queue and share the same trace.
Active station, numpad, and fallback stay visible in the live demo.
Operations can already be presented as a serious product for clinics.

lanes.title: The room understands the call and the patient sees progress
steps: The display publishes the right call with visible privacy.
The patient's phone follows the same queue without crossed messages.
Waiting feels guided because the next step stays readable.

lanes.title: Leadership can already decide with evidence instead of intuition
steps: The dashboard summarizes wait time, no-show, and room load without another report.
Aurora Derm appears as a demonstrable pilot site, not an abstract promise.
The conversation moves from visual taste to budget with purpose.

surfaces.title: The landing sells the promise in the right order.
surfaces.deck: First the operating layer becomes clear. Then the patient experience becomes tangible. Finally the dashboard helps close budget conversations.

cards.title: Patients confirm from QR or direct link
cards.copy: This is the first tangible proof that arrival can happen without putting more pressure on reception.

mockup.title: Check-in ready
mockup.footer: The same queue continues in-room and at reception.

cards.title: Patients can follow progress from the phone
cards.copy: Waiting stops feeling blind because the patient can also understand what is happening.

mockup.title: Site queue
mockup.footer: Ideal for QR on reminders, lobby, or printed ticket.

cards.title: The owner sees the KPI story behind renewal
cards.copy: The deal stops living only at front desk level once management can see wait time, no-show, and served volume.

mockup.title: Daily summary
mockup.footer: A site view first, a small-chain view next.

integrations.title: The integration story should read just as clearly
integrations.deck: Buyers need to understand in minutes what enters the pilot, what connects on day one, and what can scale later without rebuilding the base.

pricing.title: Clear packages that sell value before complexity
pricing.deck: The decision should feel simple: start with waiting-room order, add patient visibility, and close with management insight.

plans.name: Room Base
plans.fit: Small practices that need waiting room order and front desk relief.

plans.name: Flow Plus
plans.fit: Clinics with active booking, reminders, and a need for patient visibility.
features: Mobile check-in by QR or direct link

plans.name: Lite Multi-site
plans.fit: Growing small chains that need branding, roles, and site KPI visibility.

security.title: Visible privacy and dependable operations from day one
security.deck: Trust does not come from an abstract technical promise. It comes from better screens, clearer roles, and continuity when the room is under pressure.

cards.title: Privacy-safe display
items: TV shows ticket, initials, and consult room
No full phone number or full patient name outside admin
Clear state for staff, patient, and room

cards.title: Lite roles, branding, and sites
items: Reception, admin, and management with separate access
Basic branding per clinic or small chain
Prepared for 1 to 5 sites without enterprise overhead

cards.title: Operational continuity
items: Offline outbox in kiosk
TV snapshot and visible reconnection state
Reception can keep operating and reprint tickets

items.question: Do we need proprietary hardware to launch?
items.answer: No. The pilot is designed to work with a browser, a TV or monitor, a PC or tablet, and a compatible thermal printer.

items.question: Does this replace the clinic's full medical software?
items.answer: Not at this stage. The suite competes as waiting room operations + booking + reminders + reporting, not as a full EMR.

items.question: Can we start with a single site?
items.answer: Yes. The first model is built for a pilot site, then expands to branding, lite multi-site, and dashboard layers only if the flow proves itself.

items.question: How do you reduce no-show if patients never touch the kiosk?
items.answer: The improvement does not depend on the kiosk alone. It comes from reminders, mobile check-in, queue visibility, and a front desk that sees the same state.

finalCta.title: If arrival gets clearer, reception operates better, and management can measure it, the product story is already complete.
finalCta.deck: The March 17 presentation should read that way: product first, Aurora Derm as a demonstrable pilot clinic, and a journey that can be opened end to end without depending on the blocked remote.

hero.deck: The dashboard turns what happened in the room into a decision: wait time, no-show, consult room demand, and a clearer case for renewal or expansion.

steps.title: Questions leadership wants answered without asking for another report
items: How long patients wait today and where the queue is getting stuck.
How much no-show is recovered through reminders and pre-arrival check-in.
Which consult room or time block needs operational support.

advantages.title: The dashboard shifts the conversation from daily use to investment

cards.title: Front desk no longer buys alone
cards.copy: Management can see wait-time reduction, no-show improvement, and room load before deciding to expand the suite.

cards.title: Lite multi-site with purpose
cards.copy: There is no need to jump into enterprise. Comparing 1 to 5 sites on the same base dashboard is enough.

cards.title: A cleaner roadmap
cards.copy: Branding, roles, and site layers appear as the next natural step instead of as vague promises.

connections.title: These indicators exist because the earlier flow already leaves usable trace data
items: Kiosk and mobile check-in

Waiting room display call timing
Web booking and pre-arrival confirmations

finalCta.title: Now the suite reads like a complete product
finalCta.deck: A clear promise, a smoother arrival, visible waiting, and management insight. That continuity is what closes more of the gap.
